_comment: Model matrix for the benchmark harness. Add a block to benchmark a new model; no code change needed. 'key' is the LM Studio model key passed to `lms load`. 'hf_repo' is the source for downloading additional quants via `lms get <repo>@<quant>`. 'quants' are the quant tags to sweep (must be downloaded, or listed for `lms get`). 'contexts' are context lengths to test. 'mtp' adds --speculative-draft-mtp. 'thinking' is held constant per model: 'allow' or 'no_think'.

models.note: Primary target. MoE 35B total / ~3B active; ships an MTP (multi-token-prediction) head for speculative decode. Sweep quants to find the largest that fully fits your VRAM at 128k ctx.

Agentic-coding MoE, 30B total / ~3B active, 256K ctx (1M via YaRN), native tool-call format. NO MTP head (Qwen3-gen, not 3.6), so no speculative speedup on this box — expect ~no-MTP decode. Non-thinking instruct model. Smaller than the 35B (17.7GB Q4) and no vision projector.

Dense 9B, fits 8GB VRAM at Q4 (~6GB) -> fully GPU-resident. Note: dense reads ALL weights/token, so on a bandwidth-weak GPU it is not dramatically faster than the A3B MoE. Multimodal (mmproj available, disable for text).
Dense 9B agentic-coding reasoning model (deepreinforce-ai), <think> by default. Q4_K_M ~5.6GB fits 8GB VRAM. An MTP GGUF variant exists (protoLabsAI) to test speculative decode on a resident 9B.

Agentic-coding MoE, ~80B total / ~3B active (512 experts, 10 used + 1 shared), 48 blocks, hybrid Gated-DeltaNet linear attention (only 2 KV heads -> tiny KV cache, 256k native ctx). UD-Q2_K_XL (26.8 GB) fits 2x16GB fully resident. GGUF has no bundled MTP head. Non-thinking instruct. DeltaNet layers cost more GPU compute per token than plain attention, so resident decode is slower than qwen3-coder-30b despite similar active params.

OpenAI gpt-oss. MoE 117B total / 5.1B active, 36 layers, 128 experts top-4. Experts are native MXFP4, so Q8_0 (63.4 GB, attention at Q8) is effectively the only quant. Does NOT fit 32 GB VRAM -> hybrid is mandatory: cpu_experts down from 1.0, keep_in_ram=false, mmap=true, flash on. DANGER: a full-GPU or big-ctx/no-flash load overflows VRAM into CUDA sysmem fallback (committed host RAM) and can hard-lock a 64 GB box. Run ramguard.ps1 during loads.

Hybrid Mamba-2 + LatentMoE, 121B total / ~12B active (22 of 512 experts + 1 shared), 88 blocks. BF16-trained so quants scale for real (52.7 GB IQ1_M to 128 GB Q8_0) - but the dense/Mamba floor keeps even 1-bit at ~53 GB, so it's always hybrid on <=32 GB VRAM, and 12B active makes hybrid decode slow (~2x the bytes/token of gpt-oss-120b). The unsloth GGUF does NOT bundle the MTP head - LM Studio rejects draftMtp. Same OOM guardrails as gpt-oss-120b apply.
Small model used to prove the harness is model-agnostic (fits fully on a small GPU).
